title: Concordia Reference
description: Canonical attestation-level reference object. A structured pointer with a typed relationship to another signed artifact. See SPEC.md §11.5 for the full normative semantics, including the layering boundary that distinguishes attestation-level references (this schema) from envelope-level references. v0.5 ratifies the shape introduced in v0.4.0.

id.description: Identifier of the referenced artifact. URN-shaped where possible (see SPEC §11.5.7). Free-form non-empty strings are accepted for backward-compatibility.

type.description: Kind of artifact referenced. Canonical emit vocabulary is receipt, chain_session, predicate, mandate. Read-side validators accept non-empty strings and MUST preserve unknown values as opaque strings per SPEC §11.5.5 and §11.5.8 forward-compat.

relationship.description: Semantic relationship per SPEC §11.5.5. Canonical emit vocabulary is supersedes, extends, fulfills, references. Read-side validators accept non-empty strings and MUST preserve unknown values as opaque strings per SPEC §11.5.8 forward-compat.

version.description: Optional. Version of the referenced artifact when known.

signed_at.description: Optional. Timestamp of the referenced artifact's signature when known.

signer_did.description: Optional. DID of the signer of the referenced artifact when known.

extensions.description: Optional. Forward-compatibility map for v0.x extension keys. Implementations SHOULD preserve unknown keys verbatim across roundtrips.
